central_question: How do human beings perceive virtue and vice?
historical_contribution: Made inner moral responsiveness and benevolence central
limitation_or_risk: Can leave development culture and disagreement underexplained

central_question: How do sympathy sentiment and spectatorship shape moral life?
historical_contribution: Linked morality to feeling social appraisal and self-evaluation
limitation_or_risk: Can understate principle and institutional power if read too narrowly

central_question: How do duty principle dignity and universality bind moral agents?
historical_contribution: Preserved critique of sentiment convention and partiality
limitation_or_risk: Can become abstract or detached from relational care

major_figures_or_traditions: Evolutionary thought, social theory, character traditions
central_question: How do cooperation character habit and society shape morality?
historical_contribution: Connected moral agency to biological social and historical formation
limitation_or_risk: Can overgeneralize civilizational or evolutionary narratives

central_question: How does moral reasoning develop across childhood and adolescence?
historical_contribution: Made moral development and justification structure central
limitation_or_risk: Can privilege justice reasoning and verbal abstraction

major_figures_or_traditions: Carol Gilligan, care ethics, social domain theory
central_question: What does justice-only stage theory miss?
historical_contribution: Expanded moral psychology to care relationship domain differentiation and context
limitation_or_risk: Can be oversimplified as care versus justice

central_question: How do people do harm while preserving moral self-image?
historical_contribution: Explained self-regulation rationalization dehumanization and disengagement
limitation_or_risk: Can focus on mechanisms without enough historical context

central_question: How do intuition emotion and controlled cognition interact in judgment?
historical_contribution: Recentered intuition affect neuroscience and experimental dilemmas
limitation_or_risk: Can oversimplify fast emotion versus slow reason

major_figures_or_traditions: Experimental philosophy, social psychology, cognitive science
central_question: How can moral judgment be operationalized and tested?
historical_contribution: Linked philosophy and psychology through controlled empirical work

central_question: How does moral agency operate in structured worlds?
historical_contribution: Connects morality to culture politics institutions platforms and power
limitation_or_risk: Can become too broad without clear methods
